nav.settingsUnseenErrors: You have new error logs — open Settings → Data → Diagnostics

focus.title.desc: Full-screen, one task at a time
sidebar.updateInstallConfirmMessage: Lorvex will download the new version, restart, and reopen on this view. Unsaved drafts in popovers may be lost.
sidebar.createFirstListHint: Group related tasks — work, home, a project. You can rename or recolor later.
focus.peerActive.hint: Starting a session here runs in parallel — minutes are tracked per device and won't overwrite your other device's timer.
today.noFocusPlan: No focus plan set for today
today.overdueAdvice: Review these and either complete, reschedule, or defer them.
today.askAssistant: Pick a task from Upcoming or Someday to focus on today, or ask your AI assistant to plan.
today.emptyHint: If this seems wrong, refresh your data or reopen the app.
today.loadFailed: Could not load your today view.
today.loadFailedHint: This is usually temporary. Your data is safe.
eisenhower.title: Eisenhower Matrix
eisenhower.subtitle: Prioritize by urgency and importance
eisenhower.thresholdExplain: Tasks due within the threshold number of days (or overdue) are classified as "urgent". Tasks without a due date are not urgent.
eisenhower.notUrgentImportantHint: Important but not urgent. Plan deliberate slots.
eisenhower.urgentNotImportantHint: Urgent but lower importance. Minimize attention cost.
eisenhower.notUrgentNotImportantHint: Neither urgent nor important. Trim or postpone.
eisenhower.sameImportance: Urgency is based on due date — adjust the due date to change urgency
eisenhower.dragHint: Drag or press Ctrl+Arrow to move tasks between quadrants
kanban.title: Kanban Board
kanban.subtitle: Visualize tasks by status
deps.title: Dependencies
deps.emptyHint: Tasks with dependency relationships will appear here.
welcome.title: Welcome to Lorvex
welcome.description: Lorvex is an AI-powered planning system. Your AI assistant manages tasks via MCP tools, and this app is your dashboard.
welcome.step1auto: Tip: Open Settings → Assistant MCP to copy the server config for your AI assistant.
welcome.step2title: 2. Send the setup prompt to your AI assistant
welcome.step2detail: In Settings → Assistant MCP, click "Copy setup prompt" and paste it to your AI assistant so it learns your Lorvex workflow.
welcome.step3title: 3. Let your AI assistant set you up
welcome.step3detail: Tell it: "Let's set up Lorvex" — it will ask about your working hours, projects, and priorities.
welcome.exampleTaskPrompt: add a task to walk the dog tomorrow
welcome.privacyNote: Your tasks stay on this device. No telemetry. Sync is optional and uses the folder/provider you choose.
welcome.offline.banner: You’re offline — that’s fine. Lorvex works completely offline; sync is optional and can be configured from Settings.
welcome.offline.learnMoreAria: Learn more about using Lorvex offline
task.title: Task title
tasks.bulkSelectHint: Exit bulk-select (Esc) to use task shortcuts
task.confirmCancel: Cancel this task? It can be reopened later.
task.cancelSeriesConfirmMessage: This cancels every future occurrence of this recurring task. The current occurrence can still be undone individually.
task.confirmPermanentDelete: Permanently delete this task? This cannot be undone.
task.notFoundHint: It may have been deleted or moved.
task.loadFailedHint: This is usually temporary. Try again in a moment.
task.aiNotesReadOnlyHint: Written by the AI assistant. Not user-editable — ask the assistant to update.

focus.title: Focus Mode
focus.noPlan: No focus plan set for today.
focus.askAssistant: Pick a task from Today, Upcoming, or Someday — or ask your AI assistant to plan.

focus.autoSaveFailed: Focus time could not be saved — elapsed minutes may be lost if the app quits
focus.resumePromptMessage: You had a focus session running on "{title}" with {elapsed} elapsed. Pick up where you left off?
focus.breakSubtitle: Rest your eyes and stretch. The next task will begin when the timer ends.
focus.skipBreakConfirmMessage: Breaks help sustain focus across the session. Skip anyway?
capture.firstTaskCelebration: First task saved: "{title}". Press {paletteShortcut} for the command palette or ? for all shortcuts.
tags.autocomplete.label: Matching tags
capture.listRequiredHint: Choose a real list or configure a default list before creating a normal task.

quickCapture.discardConfirmMessage: You have unsaved changes. Closing now will lose them.
popover.title: Today at a glance
palette.recentUndo.label: Undo: {label}
palette.exportDataDesc: Save a portable snapshot of every task, list, habit, calendar event, AI note, and preference to a ZIP archive.
palette.importDataDesc: Restore a Lorvex export. Existing entries with matching IDs are merged via the standard sync rules; conflicting fields prefer the imported value.
palette.importDataConfirm: Importing merges the chosen archive into your local database. Continue?
palette.syncNowDesc: Push pending local changes and pull remote updates from the configured backend.
palette.resetSyncConfirm: Reset every outbox entry’s retry counter to 0 so the next sync cycle replays them. Use this when sync has stalled after a transport switch.
palette.permanentDeleteTaskConfirm: This deletes the task and every checklist item, attachment, dependency, recurrence rule, and AI changelog entry tied to it. Cannot be undone.
palette.toggleLanguageDesc: Cycle through registered interface languages without leaving the palette. The full language picker lives in Settings.
palette.cycleThemeDesc: Step through the three appearance modes. Per-mode tuning lives in Settings → Appearance.
palette.moveTaskHintDesc: Search for a task, then press Tab to pick a destination list.
palette.purgeCancelledConfirm: Permanently delete every task in the Cancelled state on this device. They will not be restored by the next sync run.
palette.deleteAllDataDesc: Open the typed-confirmation flow that wipes every local task, list, habit, calendar event, AI memory, sync cursor, and changelog. Sync folders and external provider copies are not deleted.
changelog.title: Activity Log
changelog.subtitle: All changes to your data — manual, AI, and sync.
changelog.emptyHint: Every change to your tasks, lists, and habits is recorded here.
changelog.clearConfirm: Clear all AI activity entries? This cannot be undone.

someday.subtitle: Ideas and shelved tasks

recurring.subtitle: Every task with an active recurrence rule

allTasks.bulkConfirmMoveMessage: {count} tasks will be moved to a different list — continue?

allTasks.emptySearchHint: Try a different keyword or clear the search filter.
allTasks.loadFailedHint: The task list may be temporarily unavailable.
memory.title: AI Memory
memory.subtitle: Notes saved for future AI context
memory.emptyHint: Use “+ Add memory” to seed a fact yourself, or let your AI assistant add notes as you chat.
memory.addMemoryTooltip: Add a memory the AI will see next session
memory.addMemoryFormHint: Seed a fact or preference for future AI sessions. You can edit or delete it later.
memory.valuePlaceholder: What should the AI know? (e.g. “I work 9–5 Pacific and dislike morning meetings.”)
memory.formDiscardConfirmMessage: You have unsaved changes. Closing now will lose them.
memory.lockedDescription: Your AI memory stays sealed until you confirm you're at the keyboard. Unlocking re-enables read access for this window.
memory.lockedWhyHint: Locked because biometric memory-lock is on in Settings → Privacy.
memory.notesForAiEmpty: No notes yet. Add notes to provide context for future AI conversations.
memory.aiGeneratedDesc: Things your AI assistant has learned about you from conversations. You can review, edit, or delete any of them.
memory.forgetConfirmMessage: The AI will no longer have access to this memory entry. You can restore it from the history later.
memory.deleteNotesConfirmMessage: Your notes will be removed. You can restore them from the history later.
memory.historyLoadError: Couldn’t load history. Please try again.
settings.memoryLockDescBiometric: Require biometric authentication to view AI notes. Auto-locks when you switch away.
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Preferences
settings.hint: Manage your preferences here. If you've connected an AI assistant via MCP, it can read and configure many of these too — see the Activity Log for what it has changed.
settings.workingHoursDesc: Used to schedule tasks within productive hours and avoid suggesting work outside them. An AI assistant connected via MCP reads this too.
settings.quietHoursDesc: Suppress all notifications during this time window. Supports overnight spans (e.g. 22:00 to 07:00).
settings.mutedListsDesc: Notifications for tasks in muted lists will be silenced. Badge count also excludes muted lists.
settings.focusBreakDesc: Show a break timer between tasks in Focus Mode.
settings.focus.confirmationsDesc: Control the confirmation dialogs and alerts that appear during a focus session.
settings.focus.confirmSkipBreak.label: Confirm before skipping a break
settings.focus.confirmSkipBreak.descript: Ask before ending a break early so you don't dismiss it by accident.
settings.focus.confirmExit.label: Confirm before exiting Focus Mode
settings.focus.confirmExit.description: Ask before leaving Focus Mode mid-session — useful when timers and shortcuts overlap.
settings.focus.breakEndAlert.label: Alert when a break is ending
settings.focus.breakEndAlert.description: Play a chime, flash the window, and post a system notification a few seconds before your break ends.
settings.focus.opacity.label: Focus window transparency
settings.focus.opacity.description: Lower values let what's behind the focus window show through. 100% is fully opaque.
settings.keyboardShortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
settings.keyboardShortcuts.description: Open the keyboard-shortcut cheat sheet. Also available from the sidebar Help menu or the ⌘+/ shortcut.
settings.launchOnLoginDesc: Start Lorvex automatically when you log in to your computer.
settings.menuBarIconDesc: Show or hide the Lorvex icon in your system menu bar.
settings.menuBarToggleRollback: Menu bar icon update failed. Reverted to previous state.
settings.systemTrayIconDesc: Show or hide the Lorvex icon in your system tray.
settings.systemTrayToggleRollback: System tray icon update failed. Reverted to previous state.
settings.desktopCloseActionDesc: Choose whether closing the main window quits the app or hides it to tray/menu bar.
settings.languageDesc: Display language for the app interface.
settings.sidebarModulesDesc: Choose which navigation modules appear in the sidebar.
settings.sidebarModulesHint: Changes apply immediately and are saved as `sidebar_visible_modules`. An AI assistant connected via MCP can also configure this preference.
settings.sidebarNeedOnePrimary: Keep at least one primary navigation item visible.
settings.hideEmptyLists: Hide lists with no open tasks
settings.workflowScheduleDesc: Working hours, focus limits, and calendar preferences.
settings.habitsSectionDesc: Daily reminder slots for your habits. Manage them here, or via an AI assistant connected through MCP.
settings.desktopBehaviorSectionDesc: Launch, tray icon, close action, and privacy settings.
settings.dangerZoneIntro: Every action on this page permanently removes data on this device. They are consolidated here so you (or the AI assistant) always know where to find them.
settings.dangerClearErrorLogsDesc: Deletes every entry in the local error log. Recent Logs below still aggregates AI changelog and sync events; only the error-log stream is cleared.
settings.dangerClearChangelogDesc: Removes the entire history of AI assistant writes. Tasks, lists, and calendar events are NOT touched — only the audit trail.
settings.dangerClearChangelogConfirm: Delete every AI changelog entry on this device? This cannot be undone.
settings.dangerPurgeCancelledDesc: Permanently deletes every task in the Cancelled state. They will not be restored by the next sync run.
settings.errorLogsClearMoved: Clearing error logs moved to Danger Zone below.
settings.purgeCancelledMoved: Clearing cancelled tasks moved to Danger Zone below.
settings.dangerResetPrefsDesc: Resets all settings (theme, language, working hours, sync config) to defaults. Your tasks and data are not affected.
settings.dangerResetAllDesc: Permanently deletes all local tasks, lists, calendar events, habits, plans, reviews, preferences, AI memory, sync cursors, and changelog on this device. Sync folders and external provider copies are not deleted. This cannot be undone.
settings.dangerResetAllConfirmPrompt: Type DELETE to confirm. This will erase everything.
settings.dangerResetAllConfirmMismatch: Type the exact confirmation token to continue.
settings.diagnosticsSectionDesc: Error logs and recent system activity.
settings.retentionSectionDesc: Lorvex keeps an AI changelog of every change your assistant made and a persistent error log. These grow over time. Pick how long each is kept; sweeps run when the app launches. Everything not listed here (tasks, memory, lists) is retained indefinitely.
settings.purgeCancelledConfirm: Permanently delete all cancelled tasks? This cannot be undone.
retention.hideCompletedAfter.label: Hide completed tasks older than:
retention.hideCompletedAfter.hint: Completed tasks are kept forever for audit history. This controls what's shown in list views only; clear via Settings → Data.
settings.trashSectionDesc: Tasks you delete land here first. Entries older than 30 days are automatically removed the next time Lorvex launches; you can restore or delete forever at any time.
settings.trashDeleteForeverConfirm: Permanently delete this task? This cannot be undone.
settings.trashEmptyAllConfirm: Delete every task older than 30 days from the Trash? This cannot be undone.
settings.aiBriefingDesc: Show daily briefing and schedule suggestions from AI.
settings.advancedDesc: Power-user controls and AI behavior defaults.
settings.calendarSubRemoveConfirmMessage: This will stop syncing events from {url}. Imported events on your calendar will be removed on the next refresh.
settings.calendarSubEmpty: No calendar subscriptions. Add a .ics URL to import external calendar events.
settings.calendarSubErrorNetwork: Could not reach the calendar server
settings.calendarSubErrorTooLarge: Calendar feed is too large to import
settings.calendarSubErrorInvalidIcs: Calendar feed is not a valid iCalendar file
settings.syncDesc: Local change events retained for backup and future provider-neutral sync.
settings.syncFilesystemBridgeLookbackKno: Folder lookback known-id skip metric malformed
settings.syncMethodSharedFolderDesc: Point Lorvex at a folder that Dropbox, Syncthing, a network share, or another provider keeps in sync across machines. Works cross-platform. Data lives in whichever provider hosts the folder.
settings.syncSharedFolderPathRequired: Set a shared folder path first
settings.seedFullSyncSuccess: Enqueued {tasks} tasks and {lists} lists for sync
settings.seedFullSyncHint: Upload all existing tasks and lists to the cloud (first-time setup)
settings.syncOfflinePreflight: You're offline — connect to the network and try again.
settings.syncOfflineTooltip: You're offline. Sync will resume when your connection is restored.
settings.syncOfflineHint: You're offline — try again when connected.
settings.exportScopeScopedHint: Choose the top-level data domains to include in this ZIP export.
settings.exportPickScope: Pick at least one export category
settings.syncNotAvailableOnDevice: Sync is not available on this device.
sync.errors.offline: You're offline. Try again when connected.
sync.errors.permissions: Can't access the sync folder. Check folder permissions.
sync.errors.timeout: Network timeout. Retry when your connection is stable.
sync.errors.changeTokenExpired: Sync state is out of date. A full resync is needed.
settings.syncReseedRequiredHint: Some changes have aged past the merge horizon and can no longer be applied incrementally. Export your data or re-seed from another device to restore sync.
settings.mcpConnectDesc: Lorvex uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) to connect with AI assistants. Copy the server config below into your AI assistant's MCP settings.
settings.mcpStatusServerError: MCP server reported a setup issue.
settings.mcpConfigInstruction: If this file doesn’t exist, create it. If it already has an `mcpServers` key, merge the block below into it — don’t overwrite. Restart the client after saving.
settings.mcpConfigInstructionCodex: Append this block to the file. Codex picks it up on next launch; no restart needed.
settings.mcpClaudeCodeCwdWarning: Some CLI MCP clients ignore `cwd`. If startup fails, switch to an absolute `lorvex-mcp-server` binary path config (see setup guide).
settings.mcpCopyPromptDesc: Paste this prompt to any AI coding agent to auto-configure Lorvex MCP on your machine.
settings.mcpHostCliDesc: Your AI assistants connect through the CLI. To reconfigure, run:
settings.mcpHostCliDetected: CLI installed — for headless agent runtime, run:
settings.mcpSetupHint: Use these values when available. If snippets are unavailable, follow the setup guide for manual MCP configuration.
settings.mcpSetupPrompt.template:
I need you to configure the Lorvex MCP server on my local machine. Lorvex is my AI-native personal planning system. Its MCP server lets you manage my tasks, lists, calendar, current focus, and focus schedules.

## Server Details

- **Name/key**: `lorvex`
- **Command**: `{command}`
- **Args**: [{argsStr}]
- **Transport**: stdio (the client launches the binary; no daemon or network port){cwdNote}

## What To Do

Check which of these MCP client config files exist on my machine, then add the `lorvex` server entry to each one:

### Claude Desktop (JSON)
- **Config file**: `{claudeDesktopPath}`
- **Format**: Add to the `mcpServers` object:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lorvex": {
      "command": "{jsonCommand}",
      "args": [{argsStr}]
    }
  }
}
```

### Claude Code (JSON)
- **Config file**: `{claudeCodePath}`
- **Format**: Add to the `mcpServers` object:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lorvex": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "{jsonCommand}",
      "args": [{argsStr}]
    }
  }
}
```

### OpenAI Codex (TOML)
- **Config file**: `{codexPath}`
- **Format**: Add a `[mcp_servers.lorvex]` section:
```toml
[mcp_servers.lorvex]
command = {codexCommand}
args = [{codexArgsStr}]
startup_timeout_sec = 20
tool_timeout_sec = 120
```

## Rules

- **Merge, don't overwrite.** If other MCP servers are already configured, keep them.
- **Create the config file** if it doesn't exist yet but the client is installed.
- If you cannot access my local filesystem, show me the exact content to paste and tell me which file to edit.
- After configuring, verify by calling the `get_overview` tool — it should return my task lists and stats.
settings.mcpSetupPrompt.cwdNote: If the config format supports a `cwd` field, set it to: {cwd}
settings.yourDataDesc: All your data is stored locally in SQLite. Nothing is sent to any server.
settings.exportZipHelper: Exports are always saved as ZIP archives (.zip); the extension is added automatically if you leave it off.
settings.errorLogsDesc: Client/runtime errors are accumulated here for troubleshooting.
settings.errorLogsEmptyHint: When runtime errors happen, they will appear here.
settings.errorLogsScopeHint: Clearing this only removes error_log records. Recent Logs still includes AI and sync events.
settings.recentLogsDesc: Merged diagnostics stream from error logs, AI changelog, and sync events.
settings.recentLogsEmptyHint: AI actions and sync events will show up here when available.
settings.recentLogsRetrySyncEntryRefresh: Sync entry retry reset, but diagnostics refresh failed
diagnostics.conflictLog.title: Sync conflict log
diagnostics.deviceScope.label: Device scope
diagnostics.timeWindow.label: Time window
diagnostics.exportBundle.title: Export diagnostic bundle
diagnostics.exportBundle.desc: One-click ZIP with error logs, recent AI changelog, sync conflicts, and system info — handy to attach to a bug report.
diagnostics.exportBundle.scope: Excludes task bodies, checklist items, and AI notes. Free-form text is redacted before export.
diagnostics.exportBundle.success: Bundle saved — {errors} error log(s), {changelog} changelog row(s), {conflicts} conflict row(s).
settings.aboutText: AI-native personal planning system. Manage tasks, plans, and scheduling directly here — or connect an AI assistant via MCP to have it read and write alongside you.
settings.aboutBuildCommitLink: View this build's commit on GitHub
settings.clipboardCopyHint: Select the text and press Cmd+C / Ctrl+C manually.
review.title: Weekly Review
review.loadFailedHint: Review data is temporarily unavailable. Your tasks are safe.
review.confirmShelveToSomeday: Move all active tasks in this list to Someday?

review.inactiveHint: No activity in the past week
review.allClearHint: This view summarizes completed tasks, overdue items, and stalled lists at the end of each week. It fills in as you finish tasks.
review.nothingAhead: Nothing scheduled for next week yet.
dailyReview.title: Daily Review
dailyReview.subtitle: Your day, captured.
dailyReview.emptyHint: Write a few lines about your day below — or ask your AI assistant to log it for you.
dailyReview.addReviewTooltip: Copy a prompt to ask your AI assistant to log today’s review
dailyReview.addReviewPromptSnippet: Please write today’s daily review for me. Use the `daily_review_upsert` MCP tool. Read my completed tasks, focus time, mood/energy, and calendar events for today, then summarize wins, blockers, and learnings in the `summary`, `wins`, `blockers`, and `learnings` fields.
dailyReview.addReviewPromptCopied: Prompt copied — paste it into your AI assistant
dailyReview.summaryRequired: A summary is required to save your entry.
dailyReview.draftRestored: Restored your unsaved draft from last session
calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.dayTimelineKeyboardHint: Press Command+Shift+ArrowUp or Control+Shift+ArrowUp to move earlier; Command+Shift+ArrowDown or Control+Shift+ArrowDown to move later by 15 minutes. Press Enter to open.
calendar.emptyMonthHint: Nothing scheduled this month. Connect your Mac / Windows calendar in Settings → Calendar, or set due dates on tasks to see them here.
calendar.offline.emptyState: Connect a calendar when you’re online. Lorvex keeps working without network — set due dates on tasks to see them here in the meantime.
calendar.loadFailedHint: Tasks or events could not be fetched right now.
calendar.deleteEventConfirmMessage: This event will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
calendar.recurrenceScope.editSubtitle: How would you like to apply your changes?
calendar.recurrenceScope.deleteSubtitle: How much of the series would you like to delete?

calendar.invalidDateRange: End date cannot be before start date
calendar.missingStartDate: Pick a start date before saving
calendar.invalidTimeRange: End time must be after start time for same-day events
calendar.nothingToTruncate: No future occurrences to update — this series has already finished
undo.replacementCleanupFailed: Original restored, but the replacement couldn't be removed automatically. Please delete it manually.
calendar.timezoneChangeConfirm.title: Time zone changed
calendar.timezoneChangeConfirm.descripti: Convert times from {oldTz} to {newTz}, or keep absolute (interpret in new tz)?
calendar.keyboardHint: Press Arrow keys to reschedule, Enter to open

list.emptyHint: Type a task above, press ⌘N for Quick Capture, or ask your AI assistant.
list.deleteConfirm: Delete this list? You can only delete it after moving, cancelling, or shelving its assigned tasks.
list.reorderHint: Drag to reorder, or press Alt plus Arrow keys
common.loadFailedHint: This is usually temporary. Your data is safe.

notifications.permissionBannerMessage: System notifications are disabled — deadline reminders, habit check-ins, and at-risk alerts won’t fire.
notifications.prePromptMessage: Enable notifications so Lorvex can remind you about tasks, send morning briefings, and flag at-risk deadlines. You can change this in Settings anytime.
habits.deleteReminderConfirm: Delete this habit reminder? This cannot be undone.
habits.loadFailedHint: The habit list is temporarily unavailable. Try again.

onboarding.regressionHint: Something changed since last launch — review the steps above to make sure setup is still in good shape.
onboarding.step.mcp.title: Connect your AI assistant
onboarding.step.mcp.hint: Lorvex's AI assistant writes through MCP. Open Settings → Assistant MCP, copy the server config, and paste it into your assistant client.
onboarding.step.sync.title: Choose a sync transport
onboarding.step.sync.hint: Optional: choose a filesystem bridge if you want your tasks on another device. Lorvex works fully offline if you skip this.
onboarding.step.firstTask.title: Capture your first task
onboarding.step.firstTask.hint: Press ⌘N or click the + in the sidebar to capture a task. Tasks are created as `open` — there is no inbox gating.
onboarding.step.notifications.title: Allow notifications
onboarding.step.notifications.hint: Notifications power morning briefings and at-risk reminders. Grant permission once; everything else works without it.
onboarding.step.focus.title: Try focus mode
onboarding.step.focus.hint: Focus mode opens a minimal window for a single task. Try it once so you know where it lives — ⌘⇧F is the shortcut.
habits.aiManagedTooltip: This habit's name, cue, frequency, and target are managed by your AI assistant. Ask your assistant to update them.
aiManaged.askAssistant: Ask your AI assistant to change this
aiManaged.copyPromptAria: Copy a prompt to ask your AI assistant to change this
aiManaged.promptHabit: Update this habit (name, cue, frequency, target) to: …
calendar.recurrenceScope.thisOnly.label: This event only
calendar.recurrenceScope.thisOnly.descri: Only the selected occurrence is affected. The rest of the series stays the same.
calendar.recurrenceScope.thisAndFollowin: The series is split at this date. Earlier occurrences stay the same; this and later occurrences are changed.
calendar.recurrenceScope.allInSeries.des: The change applies to every occurrence, past and future.

settings.migration.title: App Migration
settings.migration.desc: Move all your essential data to a fresh Lorvex install — this app or the Apple edition. Export creates one portable file; import applies it into the current database.
